Beautiful strand, great color range throughout the stones, strung on fish line so held up great, n came on time n packaged well. Totally 5 stars n would recommend and buy more myself any day. Great strand of chips for Larimer.

So let me brief people on chips because I often see people complain about chip strands n seem to think they should get a strand of rich A grade stones n that's NOT what chip strands are. A hank of chip strands can even vary greatly. Size can vary greatly and chip strands are NOT nugget strands. Typically sold in small, medium, or large, n once in a while extra small being 2-3 mm or smaller. Small run about 3-4 mm, medium 4- 5 sometimes 6, n large can range about 6-8 but sometimes as low as 5mm also. There's a range in sizes that usually ranges about 3 sizes of mm in chips ROUGHLY. Because chips are just that, chips. Anything larger is classified nuggets NOT chips. Often times people do closeup pictures so you can see the chips better assuming the people who buy chips already know what they are doing n buying when they don't n then they complain cuz they didn't get nuggets. Lol.

The other thing is grade. Chips are NOT A-grade stones unless they state they are A-grade chips n rest assured you will NOT get a strand of A-grade Larimer chips for $10. The whole point n beauty of chips is that you DO GET a whole range n spectrum of color fr

top_profane.text:
There's a reason this kit is sold for fifteen bucks. It's horrid. The fit is crap. The accuracy is crap. The measurements are crap. The decals are crap.

I used more filler putty below the wings where they meet the fuselage than I ever have on any other kit. The gap was almost a quarter inch wide by nearly as deep. The landing gear struts aren't long enough to allow the finished kit to sit on both main gear wheels at the same time. It actually rests on one wheel, or the other, and the keel. The landing gear itself looks like it came from a young kid's toy plane. The crew figures aren't so much 1940s pilots as they are 1930s football players. You can see through the front of the engines, through the wings, and out through the flaps. The decals are poorly manufactured and very brittle, needing easily fifteen minutes to soak and even then they come off the backing in pieces. I used leftovers from a Tamiya Corsair and a Hobby Boss Intruder.

Mid way through the build I decided I'd work it as a restoration, not a build, and that helped my state of mind, but all in all I'd rather have my fifteen bucks back.
top_profane.title: Worst manufactured kit I have ever built!

top_rant.text:
I've been doing basic machine sewing and hand-embroidery for years, but this is my first time trying an embroidery machine. So, this review is from a beginner's perspective, without any previous machines for comparison.

The good news is, for beginners, this may look intimidating when you pull it out of the box but is relatively easy to set up! If you've never used a horizontally-set upper thread spool, that may be a small learning curve; it's awkward at first but not complicated. Because the awesome little bonus-pack included some pre-wound bobbins, I skipped over the bobbin winding instructions for now. I did not know that machine embroidery requires a different type of bobbin thread! It includes a larger spool of white bobbin thread to get you started, even without the pre-wound ones. Threading the bobbin is a piece of cake, just like my regular Brother it's a quick drop-in - just make sure the thread is going the right direction (there are diagrams on the machine, don't worry!). The Quick Reference Guide for Upper Threading is pretty confusing though, because it begins step 1 with connecting the carriage, turning it on, opening the cover, etc. So, by the time you get to actually threading the machine, the book is #10 while the corresponding numbers on the machine start with #1. Not insurmountable, but a little confusing. It has a few more steps than threading a re
top_rant.title: My First Embroidery Machine, and I Am In Love

You have to buy each cut off their website (if you bought the subscription it's actually not that bad, each design is $1 and if you max out your credit allotment, you get designs at a discounted price). There are some freebies too. I was a little intimidated by the unlimited options. Where do I go, what do I start making....?!?!?! Once I got the hang out of it and had a project lined up, it made it super easy to use and learn.

Major differences of the two:
Cricut mats are THE BEST! I use these mats in my silhouette - the firmness of the mats prevent it from slipping and sliding (which in the silhouette is THE. MOST. ANNOYING. THING. EVER.) Especially when you're down to 1 sheet of the paper you need to complete a project. Cricut mats are also less expensive than Silhouettes. Both mats are insanely sticky (too sticky) so I rub the mat on my shirt to get some lint - this makes it less sticky, and when it's NOT sticky at all, I'll use a baby wipe to clean it up a little, or use soap/water. When the mat is completely "dead" and I'm too lazy to spritz with some adhesive spray, I use it as a working mat to build my pieces on, and I don't care if I damage the mat. Paint on it, spray glue, hot glue gun, etc.

The colors were great n looked much like in the image only a bit more color in mine. Perfect either way for me because I wanted n love the whole color range.

Something people need to understand about chips is that chips are literally made from the SCRAPS that are left over from the cutting out of the A-B grade parts of any given crystal or stone. Typically fall in the C-D grade range meaning you are NOT going to get a whole lot of rich color unless they dye them and that is often done nowadays with all stones. Stones like turquoise n lapis are often made from howlite or magnesite stone. Both are stones of white in original color with dark veining with howlite being much more white in places and being they are a white porous stone makes them exceptional for using to create imitation stones such as turquoise n lapis as well as other stones because they absorb dye better. It's NOT some big deception by retailers to use such processes been going on for a long time for certain stones as the ones mentioned because mankind has pretty much raped the earth dry of good quality stones of various kinds and turquoise n lapis are 2 of them but there are many more. Both howlite n magnesite also are natural stones with healing propert

top_rant.text:
SINGER FUTURA XL-550 PROBLEMS AND FIXES.

I purchased my Singer Futura XL-550 in September 2014, but only after doing a great deal of research by reading 100’s of pro’s and con’s on the XL-400 and XL-550. The biggest complaint about the Futura was thread problems & breaking needles which I figured was operator error, that they made a mistake and blamed it on the machine. But I started having these problems right away; in the first 10 minutes I had 5 or 6 bird’s nests and that happened while I was just trying out the Futura as a sewing machine. And while trying to embroider something I broke my first needle within 2 minutes and 5 more needles in the first hour. I also had problems with the large hoop. It just would not hold the fabric in place and I was not able to embroider a simple pattern that came with the machine. Also, I would get this error message “wrong size hoop installed” about a minute after I started an embroidery project. After all the research I did, to say I was pissed is putting it mildly. I was ready to take an ax to this “machine” (I won’t write down what I really said about this machine). It turns out that my first assumption about it being operator error was wrong, and I want to APOLOGIZE to everyone that post their problems; the ones that I did not believe. I was feeling like an idiot for buying this piece of junk and that old saying of
top_rant.title: Singer Futura XL-550 problems and fixes.

I have a higher end computer with great memory and a great processor. If you have a medium to low performance computer I do not know how you can even run this software at all. This program runs SO SLOWLY after awhile that recently it literally took me 45 minutes to print one file because it rendered the file much larger than it needed to be (it had a lot of font/ words, NOT graphics) and so it took that long just to spool to my printer after freezing and crashing multiple times. MOST OF THE FITMENTS ARE QUITE EXACT, BUT NOT ALL. THE LITTLE NIBS ON THE NACELLE SUPPORTS DO NOT MATCH UP EXACTLY WITH THE TINY HOLES UNDER THE WINGS, AND YOU ARE TOLD THAT IF YOU WANT TO ATTACH THE EJECTABLE EXTERNAL FUEL TANKS, YOU HAVE TO DRILL YOUR OWN HOLES. FIN

top_five_star_obscene.text:
It's kind of hard to review feathers, feathers are feathers.

I bought these to be the hen feathers on some arrows I was fletching after removing plastic vanes. I shoot a longbow and plastic vanes don't work in a longbow where you're shooting either off your hand (traditional English style longbows) or off the arrow shelf in modern longbows and flat bows. The plastic vanes don't compress as they pass over your hand/the shelf and it kicks the arrow tail-out from the side of the bow you're shooting from. It flies off-axis and will always impact the target with the nock badly offside from the point. This means the arrow is both inaccurate and slows down much quicker than it should.

So I got some cheap arrows that were spined correctly but had plastic vanes and I wanted to fletch them with feathers for practice arrows, saving my good arrows for hunting.

Which brings us to these feathers. They are very uniformly cut, they have a good base of feather core for gluing, they are nice and straight and can be using in a fletching jig for either straight or right hand helical mounting. I have both a straight and a helical clamp but I use the straight clamp and adjust the jig to give me a very slight right hand spin using the straight clamp. I find this works well in my bow because it spins the arrow enough to stabilize it well without whirling it like
top_five_star_obscene.title: Good price on good feathers.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
There are generic cheap knitting needle tip protectors you can buy by the dozens. I have. The plastic is harder, stiffer and much less grippy than Clover's gummy-textured rubber tips.

Live and learn from my cheapness, knitter friends. Clovers don't age, dry out or let go when you really don't want a bunch of stitches escaping your needles in the project bag--like 1000 stitches of a circular lace shawl that went walkabout on me due to crappy cheap tip protectors giving up the ghost in my bag.

Cue the "Psycho" violins and blood-curdling scream.

I feel the same way about Clover stitch markers, made of the same resilient, non-crappy rubber as the tip protectors. Clovers stay in place, slide fine and don't fling themselves off the needle at the very point you're about to slip them and knit onward. I have chased too many cheap rubber markers to consider them a good bargain. I wish I could buy more Clovers in a package, but whatever. If you want to save on Clover, go to a store. I love the convenience of Prime and the quality of Clover.
Mic drop.

top_five_star_obscene.text: okaaaaaaayyyyyyyyy... it's supposed to be water proof... right !!.... short story. I purchased new floor matts for an old car. I decided to treat them up front using the 303 product. I Treated on a Thursday night - dried overnight - put matts in car. Fast forward to Monday afternoon. I drove from New Orleans to Houston and I kept being bothered by a few slivers of silver trash on my new grey matts. I kept thinking. "my passenger must have dropped trash on the floor on Sunday.. no one had sat over there today(monday) "...... I completed my journey to Houston and immediately opened the passenger door to remove the matt and dump the silver trash and other minimal debris. when I picked up the matt, the silver trash "rolled" accross the matt surface......... It was actually several beads of water, some of which were in 2 inch long strings. It had sat on the treated surface ot the matt for two days including a 6 hour ride with motion and vibration. Another strange but complimentary note, the spill was actually on an inclined portion of the matt..... I dont know how long it will last, but this stuff is certainly starting out with 5 stars...  the silver appearance was related to the water drops refracting the grey carpet.. the drops looked silver, so I thought it was silver trash.....

I moved the serger to a solid wooden desk while I was waiting for the mat to come in. The serger only vibrated a little on the solid wood desk so thought, ok, I need to change my sewing furniture. I moved it back to the crappy table, settled the mat underneath and shockingly it really did help a lot. The serger isn't bouncing all over anymore. I'm actually able to sew on it there. I think I'm still going to get a sturdier table but this inexpensive mat sure helps out in the meantime.
